name: Pachacuti Inca Yupanqui
role: 9th Sapa Inca; Founder of Tahuantinsuyo (r. 1438–1471)
quote: I was born like a flower of the field, as it flourishes so I flourished; then was I melted away like hoarfrost.

name: Francisco Pizarro
role: Spanish Conquistador; Captured Atahualpa (1532); Founded Lima (1535)
quote: I have not come here for such small things. I have come for the gold.

name: Tupac Amaru II (José Gabriel Condorcanqui)
role: Indigenous rebel leader; Led Great Andean Rebellion (1780–1781); Martyred
quote: I die, but will return as millions.

name: José de San Martín
role: Liberator of Peru; Proclaimed Independence July 28, 1821; Protector of Peru
quote: From this moment on, Peru is free and independent, by the general will of the people and by the justice of its cause.

name: Simón Bolívar
role: Liberator; Commander at Junín; Organized final Ayacucho campaign (1824)
quote: The art of victory is learned in defeat. A spark of freedom is worth more than a century of slavery.

name: Víctor Raúl Haya de la Torre
role: Founder of APRA (1924); Peru's most influential 20th-century political thinker
quote: Only the people will save the people.

name: Gen. Juan Velasco Alvarado
role: President 1968–1975; Led 'Revolutionary Government'; implemented radical agrarian reform
quote: Peasant, the landlord will no longer eat from your poverty. The land is now for those who work it.

name: Abimael Guzmán ('Presidente Gonzalo')
role: Founder & leader of Shining Path (1970–1992); Life sentence; died in prison 2021
quote: We are going through a decisive moment in history. The party directs the gun; the gun will never direct the party.

name: Alberto Fujimori
role: President 1990–2000; Convicted of crimes against humanity (2009); Died Sept 11, 2024
quote: I did what was necessary to save Peru from terrorism and hyperinflation. History will be my judge.

name: Vladimiro Montesinos
role: SIN Director under Fujimori; spy chief; convicted of corruption, human rights crimes; in prison
quote: Everything has a price. Power has a price. You just have to know who to pay.

name: Alan García Pérez
role: APRA President 1985–1990 and 2006–2011; died by suicide April 17, 2019 to avoid Odebrecht arrest
quote: I have done nothing wrong. I do not surrender to those who persecute me.

name: Alejandro Toledo
role: President 2001–2006; Peru's first indigenous president; extradited from US for Odebrecht (2023)
quote: The conquest of poverty begins with the conquest of our own fear.

name: Ollanta Humala
role: President 2011–2016; former military; leftist turned moderate; jailed briefly in Odebrecht (2017)
quote: Social inclusion is not charity. It is justice. It is a debt owed to millions of Peruvians.

name: Martín Vizcarra
role: President 2018–2020; dissolved Congress (2019); impeached Nov 2020 by 'moral incapacity'
quote: I govern for Peru, not for a political party. That is why Congress cannot control me.

name: Pedro Castillo
role: President July 2021 – Dec 7, 2022; attempted self-coup; arrested; in pretrial detention
quote: No más pobres en un país rico. No more poor people in a rich country.

name: Dina Boluarte
role: President since December 7, 2022; Peru's first female president; faces protest deaths investigations
quote: I will not resign. I was called by the Constitution to govern, and that is what I will do.

name: Keiko Fujimori
role: Three-time presidential candidate (2011, 2016, 2021); Fuerza Popular leader; faces money laundering trial
quote: I am not my father. I am my own person, with my own democratic convictions.

name: Máxima Acuña
role: Indigenous farmer activist; resisted Yanacocha gold mine; Goldman Environmental Prize 2016
quote: We don't sell our land because it gives us life. The mountains are our home.

name: Francisco Sagasti
role: Transitional President Nov 2020 – July 2021; stabilized after Merino crisis; oversaw vaccination rollout
quote: I did not seek this responsibility. I accepted it because my country needed stability in the darkest moment.

name: Gen. Antonio José de Sucre
role: Bolívar's field commander; won Battle of Ayacucho (Dec 9, 1824); completed South American independence
quote: The field of Ayacucho will tell future generations what the soldiers of the fatherland are capable of.

name: Atahualpa
role: Last ruling Sapa Inca (r. 1532–1533); won civil war against Huáscar; captured at Cajamarca; executed August 29, 1533
quote: Fill this room with gold and I shall be free — and I will give you twice as much silver. This I swear.

name: Manco Inca Yupanqui
role: Spanish-installed Sapa Inca (1533); Led Great Inca Revolt (1536); Founder of Neo-Inca state at Vilcabamba (1537–1544)
quote: The Spaniards take everything by force. There is no loyalty in their promises and no honor in their hearts.

name: Ramón Castilla y Marquesado
role: President 1845–1851 and 1855–1862; abolished indigenous tribute (1854) and African slavery (1855); guano boom architect
quote: The day slavery exists in Peru, the Constitution is merely a dead letter.

name: Almirante Miguel Grau Seminario
role: Naval hero of the War of the Pacific; captain of monitor Huáscar; killed at Battle of Angamos, Oct 8, 1879; 'El Caballero de los Mares'
quote: My duty is to my nation. I know that against such odds this fight cannot be won, but honor demands we stand.

name: Andrés Avelino Cáceres
role: Led La Breña guerrilla campaign against Chilean occupation (1881–1884); 'El Brujo de los Andes'; President 1886–1890 and 1894–1895
quote: We had no weapons or money, but we had the mountains and the Andean people — and that was enough.

name: Fernando Belaúnde Terry
role: President 1963–1968 (ousted by Velasco coup) and 1980–1985; restored democracy after 12 years of military rule; oversaw early Shining Path war
quote: Peru is a country with a thousand years of history and a glorious destiny ahead. We must believe in our people.

name: Mario Vargas Llosa
role: Nobel Laureate in Literature (2010); 1990 presidential candidate (lost to Fujimori); leading liberal public intellectual; author of La ciudad y los perros
quote: Peru is a country of opportunities that its elites have always wasted. That is our tragedy and our challenge.

name: Pedro Pablo Kuczynski (PPK)
role: President 2016–2018; Wall Street economist turned politician; resigned after granting Fujimori pardon in exchange for impeachment votes; faces corruption charges and house arrest
quote: I granted the pardon for humanitarian reasons. I see it has divided Peru, and for that I am truly sorry.
